status: documentation only; no mapper code changes
purpose: Identify safe LGBTQ / gender / rights items to broaden CD-position signal beyond the abortion battery already covered in mapper-v01-source-directionality-audit.{md,json}.
explicitly_not_in_this_audit: No turnout questions or validated voter file fields.
No demographic items where respondent answers about themselves (sexuality, transgender) — strata, not policy positions.
No engine / selector / topK / browser / dist / output / live-data / candidate / era-context edits.

items.label: Gay Marriage
items.question_wording: Do you favor or oppose allowing gays and lesbians to marry legally? (inferred from variable label + empirical 65/35 Favor/Oppose split; PDF not cached locally)
items.direction: Favor → CD low; Oppose → CD high

items.label: Anti-discrimination amendment
items.question_wording: Amend federal laws to prohibit discrimination on the basis of gender identity and sexual orientation.
items.battery_prompt: Over the past two years, Congress voted on many issues. Do you support each of the following proposals?
items.direction: Favor → CD low + gender-salience high; Oppose → CD high

items.label: Equal pay
items.question_wording: Require equal pay for women and men who are doing similar jobs and have similar qualifications.
cross_loads: moralBoundaries.gender (primary load — stronger signal for gender-boundary than CD)
items.reason_for_reduced_weight: Compromise framing — broadly popular even with conservatives (~75-80% Favor); weak CD discriminator. Stronger as a gender-boundary signal than as a CD signal.

items.label: Ban Transgender People in the Military
items.question_wording: Ban Transgender People in the Military.
items.battery_prompt: For each of the following tell us whether you support or oppose these decisions.
items.direction: Support → CD high + gender-salience high; Oppose → CD low
items.notes: Cleanest 2020 transgender-policy signal. Replaces the absent same-sex-marriage direct framing post-Obergefell.

items.label: Contraceptives access bill
items.question_wording: Prohibit government restrictions on the provision of, and access to, contraceptives.
items.battery_prompt: Do you support or oppose each of the following bills before Congress?
items.direction: Support → CD low; Oppose → CD high
items.notes: Post-Dobbs salience; first cross-cycle contraceptives item in the audited bank.

items.label: Abortion access bill
items.question_wording: Prohibit government restrictions on the provision of, and access to, abortion services.
items.direction: Support → CD low; Oppose → CD high
items.reason_for_hold: Duplicates the abortion battery (CC24_324a..d) CD signal. Including in CD-breadth composite alongside abortion battery would over-weight the abortion axis vs other CD dimensions.
items.alternative_use: Confirmation item — compute correlation between CC24_324a Support rate and CC24_340b Support rate as a sanity check. Large divergence indicates respondent confusion or routing issue.

items.label: Same-sex + interracial marriage federal recognition
items.question_wording: Require that all federal agencies recognize same-sex marriages and interracial marriages.
items.context: Refers to the Respect for Marriage Act (signed into law 2022); item asks about codification effect.
items.direction: Support → CD low; Oppose → CD high
items.notes: Direct successor to CC16_335's role. Cross-cycle parallel: 2016 CC16_335 → 2020 CC20_350a (anti-discrimination framing) → 2024 CC24_340c (federal recognition framing).

items.label: Expand access to abortion
items.question_wording: Expand access to abortion, including making it more affordable, broadening the types of providers who can offer care, and protecting access to abortion clinics.
items.battery_prompt: On the topic of abortion, do you support or oppose each of the following proposals?
items.direction: Support → CD low; Oppose → CD high
items.notes: Direction is OPPOSITE of CC24_324b/c (which are restrictions). Adds a 4th datapoint to the abortion battery that pulls in the progressive direction. Ships within the abortion composite, not as separate CD-breadth signal.

items_considered_and_rejected.reason: Demographic stratum (respondent's own orientation), not a policy position. Use only for Identity-Primary overlay.
Demographic stratum (respondent identifies as trans), not a policy position.
Survey weight column; not an item.
Salience proxy at most, not a position item.
PRO/COM/CD-light cross-load — not pure CD. Worth a separate PRO breadth audit.

2024.abortion_battery: CC24_324a (1.0×) + CC24_324c (1.0×) + CC24_324d (1.0×) [CC24_324b held — analogous to 2016 332b ambiguity]
2024.abortion_axis_duplicate_held: CC24_340b (held to avoid double-counting abortion vs other CD dimensions)

same_sex_marriage_direct_framing.2020: CC20_350a (anti-discrimination amendment — different mechanism after Obergefell)
same_sex_marriage_direct_framing.2024: CC24_340c (Respect for Marriage Act federal-recognition codification)
same_sex_marriage_direct_framing.note: Conceptually parallel but not identical mechanisms. All ship-ready at weight 1.0 in their respective cycles.

transgender_policy.note: No 2024 direct trans-policy item. Gender-identity load comes only from CC20_350a coattail effect, which doesn't extend to 2024.

contraceptives.note: First cross-cycle contraceptives item in audited bank.

abortion.2024: CC24_324a..d (already audited; new 'Expand access' item adds progressive datapoint)

recommended_next_audits: CC24_321 gun-control battery — PRO/COM-breadth focus (not pure CD).
CC*_300 series 'Most Important Problem' multi-select items — candidates for moralBoundaries.gender salience derivation.
Other 2016 CC16_350 / CC16_351 items not yet audited — may contain LGBTQ-rights bills.
ANES VCF876/VCF877 gay rights items — for any future ANES-fed mapper path.
terminology: moralBoundaries.gender is the canonical compound moral-circle boundary per ADR-006. Engagement is referenced only as a downstream concern — separate 1D continuous scalar per ADR canon — not surfaced by any item audited here. Legacy code identifiers (CC16_*, CC20_*, CC24_*) appear only as CCES variable names.

revision_history.description: Initial CD-breadth audit covering 8 columns across 2016/2020/2024 LGBTQ + gender + rights items.
